terms.definition: Starting value that initializes a pseudo-random number generator so the same sequence of random draws repeats on reruns with the same software stack. Canonical home for this concept in experiment-tracking topics.
Deterministic algorithm that produces statistically random-looking numbers from a seed. Python random, NumPy, and PyTorch each maintain separate PRNG state.
Training or evaluation executed with fixed seeds, consistent data order, stable hyperparameters, and documented hardware so reruns produce comparable results.

Recording and fixing the runtime stack — Python version, library versions, CUDA/cuDNN, container image — so code executes with the same dependencies elsewhere. Overview canonical home here; Versioning Experiments covers artifact lineage.
Parameter on splits, CV, and some estimators that fixes shuffle and subsampling behavior. Must be logged alongside global library seeds.
Ability to recreate comparable metrics from documented code, data, seeds, and environment. Distinct from repeatability (same person, same machine) but both depend on seeding and pinning.
CUDA kernel whose output can vary across runs due to parallel reduction order unless deterministic mode is enabled — a common source of seed-respecting but numerically drifting metrics.
